title: Academic Withdrawal Refunds
content:
Withdrawal and Refund Policies
Morgan State University

---

TUITION REFUND SCHEDULE

The following refund schedule applies when students officially withdraw from the University:

Time of Withdrawal | Tuition Refund | Fees Refund
-------------------|----------------|-------------
Before classes begin | 100% | 100%
During first week | 100% | 0%
During second week | 80% | 0%
Between two and three weeks | 60% | 0%
Between three and four weeks | 40% | 0%
Between four and five weeks | 20% | 0%
After five weeks | 0% | 0%

---

MANDATORY FEES

Note: Mandatory fees are non-refundable once instruction begins.

Mandatory Fees Include:
- Student Activity Fee
- Athletic Fee
- Student Union Operating Fee
- Student Union Auxiliary Fee
- Insurance Fee

---

IMPORTANT WITHDRAWAL INFORMATION

To receive any refund or adjustment, you MUST officially withdraw from the University.

IMPORTANT: Simply not attending class does NOT count as a withdrawal.

How to Officially Withdraw:
1. Contact the Office of Records and Registration
2. Complete the official withdrawal process
3. Return any university property
4. Settle any outstanding balances

---

ADDITIONAL REFUND INFORMATION

For Room & Board Refunds:
Contact the Office of Residence Life

For Changes to Financial Aid Package:
Contact the Office of Financial Aid

To Check Your Refund Status:
Visit bursar.morgan.edu

---

FINANCIAL AID IMPACT

Withdrawing from courses may affect your financial aid:
- May need to return a portion of federal aid
- May affect Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP)
- May impact future aid eligibility

Contact the Office of Financial Aid before withdrawing to understand the impact.

---

CONTACT INFORMATION

Office of Records and Registration
Office of Residence Life
Office of Financial Aid
Bursar's Office: bursar.morgan.edu
